author: Benkirane R
title: The Alchemy of Revolution: The Role of Social Networks and New Media in the Arab Spring
argument_summary: The article uses the hashtag #sidibouzid as an empirical dataset to demonstrate how the Tunisian Revolution was articulated across multiple linguistic spheres on Twitter. The hashtag functions as a connective device linking different audiences and enabling the synchronization of local events with global visibility.
relevant_quote: the top Twitter users alimenting the hashtag #sidibouzid were the spheres’ vital connectors who used multilingual communication in order to reach different audiences...

author: Galvin R
title: Fire:Agent of change,Western Oregon University
argument_summary: The author uses the progression of hashtags (#Bouazizi, #SidiBouzid, #Tunisia) to show how a local protest expanded into a national uprising, emphasizing the role of social media in spreading images, connecting social networks, and amplifying the movement.
relevant_quote: Without social media, Aljazeera would not have had any videos to show, (...) those without internet could now be included in the social network that had begun there, #SidiBouzid.

author: Poell T;Darmoni K
title: Twitter as a multilingual space: The articulation of the Tunisia Revolution through #sidibouzid
argument_summary: The authors use #sidibouzid to show how social media platforms operate as multilingual assemblages, in which diaspora networks connect different linguistic spheres and enable the transnational circulation of information. The analysis emphasizes that the hashtag was primarily used to disseminate information globally rather than to coordinate protests on the ground.
relevant_quote: our task was 'information escape', the reproduction and structuring of information,making it accessible to especially Al Jazeera(...)

author: Brydon L;Pastor-Gonzales V
title: Collaborative Futures/Futuros en Colaboración
argument_summary: The article argues that collaboration is essential to media studies, enabling the development of new approaches and the revision of established perspectives despite structural constraints. It emphasises that collaboration involves often invisible labour shaped by inequalities. Hashtags appear as editorial markers (e.g., #Solidarity, #Method) that organise thematic debates and connect different moments of academic production, contributing to disciplinary continuity over time.
relevant_quote: Informed by (separate) prior experiences of collaboration and reflective of recent work published in the journal, not least in the #Solidarity and #Method issues, we both entered our meeting ready to embrace the challenge of thinking and writing together.
